summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that FUXA v1.2.7 accepts an unauthenticated upload request whose crafted destination escapes the intended upload directory and overwrites server/main.js with attacker-controlled content.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should use POST /api/upload without credentials to overwrite a chosen application file outside the normal upload directory and leave attacker-controlled bytes on disk.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh emitted UPLOAD_POC_OK and returned {"location":"/resources/main.js"}. The verification helper confirmed that the SHA-256 hash of server/main.js changed and that the file content became evohunt-upload-overwrite.
verification_method: The verification helper starts the vulnerable server, records the original hash of server/main.js, performs the crafted unauthenticated POST /api/upload request, then requires both a changed SHA-256 digest and exact file content equality with the proof string. This distinguishes a real arbitrary overwrite from a benign upload response.
evidence_produced_by_run: STDOUT contained the proof marker UPLOAD_POC_OK.
The target file server/main.js changed hash and its content matched the attacker-controlled proof string evohunt-upload-overwrite.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
